lines.en_lit: what can be said? Do not say afterward he said. Rather, say afterward he took, and said.
taken adds and brings his obligation what does it taken
which he redeemed But there is no redemption for a bird,

Rav Pappa said: where he took one bird. If he purchased burnt-offering, add and bring his obligation from the money for his sin-offering. And this burnt-offering, goes toward a free-will offering. if he purchased sin-offering, cannot add and bring his obligation from the money for his burnt-offering. And this sin-offering, goes to death,

the itself, Rabbi Elazar said Rabbi Hoshaya said: A wealthy person who ritually impurifies the Temple, and brings offering a poor he does not fulfill Rabbi Ḥaga said Rabbi Hoshaya said: He does fulfill
raises an objection A poor leper who brings offering a wealthy person fulfills A wealthy person who brings offering a poor person does not fulfill It is different there, as it is written: “This

If so, the first clause, also The Merciful One includes “The law of” As it was taught “The law of” in order to include a poor leper who brought offering a wealthy might even a wealthy who brought offering a poor person the verse states “this”
And let us derive from the Merciful One excludes “If he be poor”
MISHNA: tied a strip of crimson upon the head of the scapegoat and positioned opposite the place from which it was dispatched, and the slaughtered, opposite place of its slaughter.

He comes next to his bull a second and places his two hands upon it, and confesses. And this is what he would say: Please God, I have sinned, I have done wrong, I have rebelled before You, I and my family and the children of Aaron, your sacred people. Please God, grant atonement, please, for the sins, and for the wrongs, and for the rebellions that I have sinned, and done wrong, and rebelled before You, I, and my family, and the children of Aaron, your sacred people, as it is written in the Torah of Moses, your servant: “For on this day atonement shall be made for you to cleanse you And they, respond after him Blessed be the name of His glorious kingdom forever and all time.
GEMARA: A dilemma was raised before them: And the slaughtered one referring to tying Or, referring to positioning

Come hear that Rav Yosef taught: He ties a strip of crimson to the head of the scapegoat and positions it opposite the place from which it will be sent; and to the slaughtered one, opposite its place of slaughter. So that each cannot become mixed up with the other and cannot become mixed up with other

Granted, if you say is referring tying, well. But, if you say it is referring positioning granted, cannot be mixed up with its counterpart, since this one, has tied to it, and that one, does not have tied to it. However, could be mixed up with other Rather, not conclude from it is referring to tying? learn from it
Rabbi Yitzḥak said: I heard two one of the heifer and one of the scapegoat. One requires amount, and one does not require amount. But I do not know which of them
Rav Yosef said: Let us see of the scapegoat, which requires division, requires amount of the heifer does not require division, does not require amount.
Rami bar Ḥama strongly objects to this: of the heifer also requires weight, Rava said to him: is tanna’im,
of the red heifer does not require division? Abaye raised an objection to How does he perform He wraps with the remnants of the strip say: with the tail end
Rabbi Ḥanin said Rav said: the cedar wood and the strip of crimson were caught by the flame it is valid. raises an objection was singed he brings another strip and sanctifies it
Abaye said: This is not difficult: Here, with blazes high. There, with blazes low,

Rava said: weight is tanna’im, as it was taught Why he wrap So that they will all be in a single bundle this is the statement of Rabbi Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, says: So that they will have weight and fall into the burning heifer.

When Rav Dimi came he said Rabbi Yoḥanan: I heard three One of the heifer, and one of the scapegoat, and one of the leper. One weight of ten zuz; and one weight of two sela, and one weight of a shekel, but I cannot explain
When Ravin came he explained in the name of Rabbi Yonatan
